groups.patch_readiness: verifier-ready patches were generated and clean replay was attempted
film_evidence: All three verifier stderr artifacts report pytest could not find tests/test_options.py::test_case_insensitive_choice_returned_exactly.
All three verifier stdout artifacts report no tests ran.
Click 005 verifier/run.sh invoked test_case_insensitive_choice_returned_exactly, but the hidden test file only defined test_case_insensitive_choice before this triage.
The generated patches all targeted click/types.py Choice case-insensitive matching and tests/test_options.py.
groups.suggests: task/verifier semantics repair before drawing model-ability conclusions from the three exit-code-4 cells
groups.not_suggested_by_film: ordinary model inability for the three patch-ready Click 005 cells

local_repair_applied.change: Added the missing test_case_insensitive_choice_returned_exactly hidden test so verifier/run.sh names existing pytest nodes and checks exact returned choice values.

groups.patch_readiness: not verifier-ready; no submission.patch was written
film_evidence: The redacted response was valid top-level v3 edit JSON, but its supplied before/after anchors did not identify the old string in click/types.py.
The old string itself occurred exactly once, which points to anchor-use brittleness rather than a missing context file.
The prompt snapshot had full Click 005 context files and statement_truncated=false.
groups.suggests: prompt/output-contract repair for anchor instructions, especially telling models to omit anchors unless they are exact immediate context
groups.not_suggested_by_film: task/verifier semantics failure; no verifier replay occurred

groups.patch_readiness: verifier-ready patches were generated and clean replay was attempted
film_evidence: Both verifier stdout artifacts show 12 failing prompt_required tests.
The shared failure begins at option construction with TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'prompt_required'.
The cheap-click-specialist patch only changed parser handling and tests, so it did not add prompt_required to Option.
The frontier-click-specialist patch added a parser sentinel and consume_value handling, but still did not add prompt_required to Option.__init__.
groups.suggests: ordinary implementation miss on Click 008; task/verifier semantics appear coherent for these two cells
groups.not_suggested_by_film: output-contract repair; both cells produced verifier-ready patches

groups.patch_readiness: not verifier-ready; no submission.patch was written
film_evidence: The cheap-generic-swe response had a unique old string in src/click/parser.py but supplied a non-matching before anchor.
The frontier-generic-swe response proposed an old string in src/click/core.py that occurred zero times.
All Click 008 prompt snapshots had src/click/core.py truncated=true, while src/click/parser.py and the test files were not truncated.
groups.suggests: prompt/output-contract and context-window repair before treating these invalid submissions as verifier-level model failures
groups.not_suggested_by_film: task/verifier semantics failure; neither cell reached verifier replay

no_more_live_spend_until: Click 005 fixed-verifier no-new-spend replay of the three existing patch artifacts is run and recorded, or the prior three failures are explicitly excluded from model ability analysis as verifier-collection artifacts.
The v3 output contract is tightened for anchor usage, and Click 008 context truncation risk is addressed for core.py before another live expansion.
priority_order: Run no-live replay for existing Click 005 patch artifacts against the repaired verifier.
Add a focused output-contract regression around unique-old plus bad-anchor behavior, deciding whether to reject bad anchors or ignore anchors when old is unique.
For Click 008, improve prompt/context packaging around prompt_required/Option.__init__ before spending on retries.

limitations: This artifact does not re-run existing patches, so it preserves the original Click 004-008 box score.
The Click 005 verifier repair changes future verifier semantics; historical normalized artifacts still record the pre-repair verifier digest and exit code 4.
Provider usage costs remain provider-usage estimates, not invoice-backed actual billed cost.
